name: The Spur Line and Ballast Path
description: A maintenance spur running north from the main, its crushed stone ballast glinting faintly in moonlight between dark wooden ties — the telegraph wire runs parallel on cedar poles, close enough to hear the wind hum through it.
atmosphericDetails: The telegraph wire hums at a low frequency in the northwest wind — almost musical, almost a warning.
The ballast is wet from yesterday's rain and the footing is uncertain in the dark.
A pocket relay tap was used here recently — the cedar pole at the quarter-mile mark shows fresh scrape marks where a clip was attached.
